rationale: Review 1 correctly finds no reuse violations in the diff (all new items extend Mathlib primitives like HopfAlgebra/FiniteType/IsGeometricallyReduced/GroupLike/IsNilpotent with no duplication). Review 2's sole finding (AlgPoints:k-specialization vs. hypothetical general form in an unshown roadmap) is ungrounded in the diff, not a reuse violation today, and changing it would be needless (or break current usage) so produces a strictly worse merged PR.
